articles.title: PM Modi urges voters to oust ‘nirmam’ TMC government from Bengal; promises six guarantees if BJP wins
articles.description: PM Modi promised that a BJP government in the State will replace the TMC's reign of 'Bhoy' (fear) with that of 'Bharosa' (trust), while all public servants will be made accountable to the people

articles.title: Thousands of Palestinians pray at Al-Aqsa Mosque after Israel’s 40-day ban
articles.description: Around 3,000 worshippers held morning prayers at Al-Aqsa after Israel lifted restrictions.

articles.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis
articles.description: The US and Israel thought they could decimate Iran. But Iran's survival - despite massive human costs - is a victory.

articles.title: Murder case involving speech-and-hearing impaired persons cracked with help from sign language interpreters in Mysuru
articles.description: Mysuru police took the accused to the Institute for Speech and Hearing in Mysuru, and interrogated with the assistance of sign language interpreters; the victim and the accused in the case were speech-and-hearing impaired

topStories.title: Quote of the day by Jim Whittaker, 1st American to summit Mount Everest: ‘When you live on the edge…’
topStories.description: Jim Whittaker, who is the first American to climb Mount Everest, wanted the readers to act.

topStories.title: Iran has been bloodied, but it is winning against the US-Israel axis
topStories.description: The US and Israel thought they could decimate Iran. But Iran's survival - despite massive human costs - is a victory.

topStories.title: US-Iran war: Tehran announces alternative routes in Strait of Hormuz amid mine threat. What are they?
topStories.description: Trump announced a fragile two-week ceasefire with Iran, allowing passage through the Strait of Hormuz. However, the main shipping lane remains dangerous due to sea mines, prompting Iran to advise alternative routes.

topStories.title: Quote of the Day: Ex-Iraqi President Saddam Hussein on world politics - ‘The west need someone to tell the man…’
topStories.description: Saddam Hussein was Iraq's President from 1979 to 2003, known for his authoritarian rule and human rights abuses. He oversaw major conflicts, was removed by a U.S.-led invasion in 2003, captured, and executed in 2006 for crimes against humanity.

topStories.title: Trump says US forces to stay near Iran, ready for ‘next conquest’
topStories.description: US threats against Iran and ongoing Israeli attacks in Lebanon cast doubt on fragile ceasefire efforts.

topStories.title: Why Lebanon Is A Bone Of Contention In Us-Iran Ceasefire Plan
topStories.description: Created by the IRGC during the Lebanese Civil War, the Shiite Muslim movement gained its moniker - 'the Resistance' - by fighting Israeli troops occupying southern Lebanon till 2000.

topStories.title: Several Tribal Leaders Defect From BJP To Congress In Gujarat's Dahod
topStories.description: Several tribal leaders from Dahod BJP unit join Congress citing dissatisfaction.

topStories.title: Opinion: Deal Or 'Setup'? Why Both US And Iran Think This Ceasefire Is A Trap
topStories.description: Both Washington and Tehran have, rather predictively, sought to frame the outcome as a victory, even as early reports of minor violations hint at the fragility of the arrangement.

topStories.title: 'Growth Shock In Making': Nomura's Rob Subbaraman Explains Global Stagflation Triggers
topStories.description: With U.S. Treasury yields already rising, Subbaraman believes it will be difficult for policymakers to continue raising rates without crushing already fragile global demand trends.

topStories.title: US Asks Allies For Quick Plans To Secure Hormuz After Ceasefire
topStories.description: The request was presented during discussions between American and NATO officials at the White House - where Trump met Rutte

topStories.title: Flip-flop on COP: Congress's dig at government on withdrawing bid to host COP33
topStories.description: It also calls into question the Modi government's true commitment to take on more ambitious carbon mitigation goals in the short and medium term, Congress leader Jairam Ramesh says

topStories.title: Did Trump's officials threaten Catholic Church? Vatican cancels White House’s invitation to host Pope Leo XIV
topStories.description: The US government under Trump threatened Catholic Church due to which Vatican postponed White House’s invitation to host Pope Leo XIV indefinitely. The Vatican viewed US official's comments as intimidation, prompting a reevaluation of diplomatic relations.

dailyNews.title: Karnataka bypolls at 9 am: 9% polling in Davanagere South, 11.87% in Bagalkot
dailyNews.description: The polls were necessitated in Bagalkot and Davanagere South by the deaths of senior Congress MLAs H.Y. Meti and Shamanur Shivashankarappa respectively

dailyNews.title: ‘Lebanon not part of the deal?’ Conflicting claims puts Iran-US, Israel truce in danger - Here's who said what
dailyNews.description: The ceasefire between Iran and the US is deteriorating as tensions rise over Israeli strikes in Lebanon. Both nations had claimed victory after agreeing to a truce, but Israel's actions have led to threats of renewed hostilities from Iran and Hezbollah.

dailyNews.title: For Gulf states, Hormuz uncertainty casts shadow over fragile US-Iran truce
dailyNews.description: Experts say Gulf states wary US could agree to deal giving Iran leverage over strategic energy waterway.

dailyNews.title: Russia offers South Asian countries sanctioned LNG at 40% discount amid war-induced energy crisis: Report
dailyNews.description: While Russia has been steadily expanding exports from its US-sanctioned export plants — Arctic LNG 2 and Portovaya — most buyers remain wary of taking restricted shipments out of fear of retaliation from Washington.

dailyNews.title: Markets Surge on Trump-Iran Ceasefire - Business Standard
dailyNews.description: Markets Surge on Trump-Iran Ceasefire  Business Standard

dailyNews.title: Trump slams NATO over Iran after meeting Rutte, renews Greenland threat
dailyNews.description: US president meets NATO chief, expresses disappointment over member states failing to back war on Iran.

dailyNews.title: Amazon's delivery battle could hit your pocket hard? What you need to know about Mamdani's new bill
dailyNews.description: Amazon is disputing a New York City bill that could require it to hire thousands of delivery workers, potentially increasing delivery costs and disrupting its current model. The bill, reintroduced by Tiffany Caban, may lead Amazon to relocate its distribution centers outside the city.

dailyNews.title: US military assets to stay around Iran until real agreement is reached: Trump
dailyNews.description: US military assets to stay around Iran until real agreement is reached Trump

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court pushes back against Centre's 'faith' argument: ‘Can’t be completely denuded'
dailyNews.description: The Centre had earlier argued that the domain of religious faith and denominational autonomy and lies beyond the scope of judicial review.

dailyNews.title: Assam election 2026 LIVE: Assam votes in high-stakes polls; BJP eyes third term as Congress-led alliance mounts challenge
dailyNews.description: Polling began at 7 a.m. and will conclude by 5 p.m. across 31,490 polling stations in 35 districts.

dailyNews.title: Trump’s last-minute delay: Why he was never going to obliterate Iran in the first place
dailyNews.description: Trump employed delay tactics to avoid following through on his pledges to relentlessly bomb Iran, following a pattern similar to his 11th-hour tariff compromises.

dailyNews.title: Michigan woman arrested for allegedly starving, torturing disabled sister-in-law she locked in basement
dailyNews.description: A Michigan woman was arrested after she allegedly kept her disabled sister-in-law locked in a basement for two years and nearly starved her to death.

topStories.description: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.

topStories.title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
topStories.description: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

topStories.title: Trump Administration Investigating L.A. Schools’ Gender Disclosure Policies
topStories.description: The investigation into the nation’s second-largest school district was prompted by a lawsuit from parents who say the policies contributed to their child’s death.

dailyNews.title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
dailyNews.description: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

dailyNews.title: Iran accuses U.S. of violating ceasefire amid Israeli attacks on Lebanon
dailyNews.description: Iran says Israel is violating the ceasefire deal President Trump announced, and Tehran appears to still have control over shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: 4/8: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: U.S.-Iran ceasefire in fragile place; NATO chief Mark Rutte meets with President Trump at the White House.

dailyNews.title: Judge postpones termination of TPS for Ethiopians in U.S.
dailyNews.description: It's the latest setback for the Trump administration on the issue, which has sought to terminate the TPS designation for 13 countries as part of the president's crackdown on immigration.

dailyNews.title: Stocks rally and oil prices drop following ceasefire announcement
dailyNews.description: Stocks jumped and oil prices fell on Wednesday following President Trump's announcement of a two-week ceasefire in the war with Iran. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. ready for 'next conquest,' warns military to remain near Iran until 'real agreement' is honored
dailyNews.description: The statement came a day after Washington and Tehran agreed to a two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan, which halted six weeks of fighting.

dailyNews.title: 'Poorly run, piece of ice': Trump targets Greenland again as Iran war deepens NATO rift
dailyNews.description: Trump has set his eyes on Greenland again while venting frustration at NATO, as the diplomatic fallout from Iran war exposed rifts in the security alliance.

dailyNews.title: Trump wants Strait of Hormuz open 'without limitation, including tolls' during Iran ceasefire: White House
dailyNews.description: Iran is reportedly planning to demand that shipping firms pay tolls in cryptocurrency to let their oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. fragile amid continued strikes
dailyNews.description: With a fragile ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran in place, Israel launched its biggest attacks yet against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon. Iran says Lebanon is part of the ceasefire, while the U.S. and Israel say it is not. The White House announced Vice President J.D. Vance will travel to Pakistan for negotiations with Iran. NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez reports.

dailyNews.title: Oil plunges on Iran ceasefire news, setting the stage for some relief on gas prices
dailyNews.description: Stocks roared higher after President Trump announced a two-week ceasefire with Iran. Oil prices fell sharply, and gas prices are expected to follow.

dailyNews.title: Strikes on Lebanon test U.S.-Israeli relations
dailyNews.description: Israeli strikes on Lebanon have reportedly stalled the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. NBC News' Matt Bradley joins "Here's the Scoop" to explain how this delay could strain the relationship between President Trump and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

dailyNews.title: White House defends Trump's 'very tough rhetoric' on Iran
dailyNews.description: NBC News’ Gabe Gutierrez questions White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt about President Trump’s Truth Social post threatening to wipe out “a whole civilization” on Tuesday.
